title: Gabor and Non-Gabor Neural Representations Are Shared between Visual Perception and Mental Imagery.

assets.abstract: Visual perception and mental imagery have been shown to share a hierarchical topological visual structure of neural representation, despite the existence of dissociation of neural substrate between them in function and structure. However, we have limited knowledge about how the visual hierarchical cortex is involved in visual perception and visual imagery in a unique and shared fashion. In this study, a data set including a visual perception and an imagery experiment with human participants was

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
